name: Gevo, Inc.

profile.description: Gevo is a renewable fuels and chemicals company developing sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and other renewable hydrocarbons from plant-based feedstocks.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): Revenue grew 695.6% YoY from a tiny base as SAF production begins scaling [1]. AI Deployment (0/2): No AI products. Market Share (2/2): Pioneer in sustainable aviation fuel with first-mover advantage in SAF technology. Pipeline (1/2): Net-Zero 1 facility construction and SAF production ramp. R&D Intensity (1/1): Heavy R&D in renewable chemistry [1].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): Specific mission to transform fuels through renewable chemistry — genuine climate-driven purpose connecting aviation to sustainability. Community (0/2): 151 employees. Retention (1/2): Small dedicated team. Insider (1/2): Some insider activity [1].

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: Deeply challenged financials. Forward P/E of -98.5 reflects massive losses. D/E of 35.6% is modest but ROE of -6.8% and profit margin of -21.1% show continued cash burn [1]. Revenue growth of 695.6% is from a tiny base. Liquidity cleared at $7.2M daily [1].

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Renewable fuel production is physical-world chemistry and manufacturing that AI cannot displace [1]. AI Opportunity (0/1): None [1].

infinite_game.summary: Gevo plays an infinite game through genuine climate-driven purpose in sustainable aviation fuel.

just_cause.analysis: Genuine cause (2→7): Decarbonizing aviation through renewable SAF [1].

existential_flexibility.analysis: Some (1→6): Pivoted from ethanol to SAF [1].

investment_thesis: Gevo is a SAF pioneer with 695.6% revenue growth, genuine climate mission, and first-mover advantage [1].
thesis_continued: Deep losses, negative forward P/E of -98.5, and pre-scale operations make this highly speculative [1].
key_risks: Massive cash burn with -21.1% profit margin [1]
SAF cost competitiveness vs. fossil jet fuel [1]
sources: Yahoo Finance / yfinance data for GEVO (Gevo), retrieved April 2026
